name: Badajoz and Cañada Real Sancha Brava Loop
name_original: Mirador de Badajoz y laguna de los Tres Arroyos — circular por la Cañada Real Sancha Brava
description: This moderate 26.7-mile mountain biking route follows the historic Cañada Real Sancha Brava, offering diverse landscapes and views of Badajo
region_intro: Mountain biking around Tierra de Badajoz offers diverse landscapes for outdoor exploration. The region features extensive pasturelands, ancient oak forests, and significant river systems like the Guadiana, providing varied terrain for mountain bike trails. Dehesas, traditional agro-silvopastoral systems with widely spaced oak trees, are also prevalent. This environment supports a network of routes that cater to different skill levels, from gentle paths along riverbanks to more challenging trails through hills. Best mountain bike trails in Tierra de Badajoz The most popular mountain bike trail is Finca las Carboneras y ermita de Bótoa – circular por la orilla del río Gévora desde Badajoz, a 29.8 miles (48.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 47 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the banks of the Gévora River, offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Embalse de Piedra Aguda – circular desde Badajoz por el Camino Natural del Guadiana y Cañada Real Sancha Brava, a moderate 42.5 miles (68.4 km) path. This route explores the natural path of the Guadiana River and the Cañada Real Sancha Brava. Local mountain bikers also love the Pueblonuevo del Guadiana y Talavera la Real – circular por la Cañada Real de las Bardocas desde Badajoz, a 33.4 miles (53.8 km) trail leading through the Cañada Real de las Bardocas, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Mountain biking around Tierra de Badajoz is defined by extensive pasturelands, ancient oak forests, and river systems. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Tierra de Badajoz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Tierra de Badajoz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Tierra de Badajoz?
A: Tierra de Badajoz offers a wide array of mountain biking opportunities, with nearly 200 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from gentle paths to more challenging ascents through diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Tierra de Badajoz?
A: The region is characterized by diverse landscapes, including extensive pasturelands, ancient oak forests, and significant river systems like the Guadiana. You'll find trails winding through traditional &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt; (agro-silvopastoral systems), along riverbanks, and through areas like the Sierra Grande de Hornachos, known for its rich ecosystem of holm oaks and cork oaks.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Tierra de Badajoz?
A: Yes, Tierra de Badajoz has 37 easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. These routes often follow flatter terrain along riverbanks or through open pasturelands, providing a gentle introduction to the region's natural beauty.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 25 difficult mountain bike routes in Tierra de Badajoz. An example is the demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032194884/sierra-de-los-picorros-sierra-mayorga-y-embalse-de-alpotrel-circular-desde-albuquerque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de los Picorros, Sierra Mayorga y Embalse de Alpotrel – circular desde Albuquerque&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes and covers over 80 km.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Tierra de Badajoz?
A: Many mountain bike routes in Tierra de Badajoz are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030811304/embalse-de-piedra-aguda-circular-desde-badajoz-por-el-camino-natural-del-guadiana-y-canada-real-sancha-brava" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Piedra Aguda – circular desde Badajoz por el Camino Natural del Guadiana y Cañada Real Sancha Brava&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the natural path of the Guadiana River.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Tierra de Badajoz?
A: Many trails offer scenic views and pass by historical and natural landmarks. For instance, routes around Badajoz city might take you near the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Palmas&lt;/a&gt;, the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Badajoz&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cathedral of Saint John the Baptist&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features natural monuments like the Caves of Fuente de León.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Tierra de Badajoz?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 200 reviews. Mountain bikers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic routes along riverbanks, and the opportunity to explore ancient oak forests and traditional &lt;em&gt;dehesas&lt;/em&gt;.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that follow the Guadiana River?
A: Yes, the Guadiana River is a prominent feature of the region, and several mountain bike routes follow its picturesque banks. You can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031497876/puente-ayuda-circular-desde-badajoz-por-el-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Ayuda — circular desde Badajoz por el río Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful riverside cycling.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Tierra de Badajoz?
A: While not explicitly stated, the mild climate of Extremadura generally makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for mountain biking. During these seasons, temperatures are pleasant, and the natural landscapes are vibrant, offering comfortable riding conditions.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate the region's reservoirs. For example, the difficult route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1032163027/embalse-de-la-pena-del-aguila-y-rincon-de-gila-vuelta-desde-badajoz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Peña del Águila y Rincón de Gila – vuelta desde Badajoz&lt;/a&gt; includes views of the Embalse de la Peña del Águila. The region also features the Orellana reservoir in La Serena, known for its freshwater blue flag beach.

Q: Can I find mountain bike trails that are less crowded or considered 'hidden gems'?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are not listed, the vast network of nearly 200 trails means you can often find quieter routes away from the most popular spots. Exploring areas like Campiña Sur de Badajoz or the Sierra Grande de Hornachos might lead you to less-trafficked paths, offering a more secluded experience amidst nature.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails near the city of Badajoz?
A: Yes, many routes start from or pass through the vicinity of Badajoz city. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1033747460/finca-las-carboneras-y-ermita-de-botoa-circular-por-la-orilla-del-rio-gevora-desde-badajoz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Finca las Carboneras y ermita de Bótoa – circular por la orilla del río Gévora desde Badajoz&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate trail that follows the banks of the Gévora River, easily accessible from the city.
tips: Along this section, we ride along the Cañada Real Sancha Brava where we will find several explanatory panels and posts. An ideal place for lovers of mountain biking and gravel.
We are in the Tres Arroyos Municipal Park, an ideal place to take a break and breathe fresh air near the city of Badajoz.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route typically takes around 2 hours and 55 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 43.0-kilometer loop, which is rated as moderate.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is rated as moderate and requires good physical fitness and prior mountain biking experience. Its technical difficulty is approximately S2 on the Single Trail Scale, meaning advanced riding skills may be necessary for some sections. While most of the trail is rideable, you might occasionally need to push your bike on harder parts.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse terrain. You'll ride along a segment of the historic Cañada Real Sancha Brava, which is often wide with good visibility. The landscape transitions from ruderal vegetation to Mediterranean scrubland and forest, featuring cork oaks, strawberry trees, rockroses, lavender, and gorse. A notable section is the 'Cuesta Bermeja,' known for its reddish, clay-rich soil. You'll also encounter dehesa landscapes, riparian forests, and crossings over temporary streams.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience the historic Cañada Real Sancha Brava. Key viewpoints include a View of Badajoz, and you can also expect views of the Cerro del Conjuro and the Sierra de San Pedro. The San Isidro Lake and the Laguna de los Tres Arroyos area are also picturesque natural features.

faqs.question: Is the Mirador de Badajoz and Laguna de los Tres Arroyos trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail follows public pathways like the Cañada Real Sancha Brava, which are generally accessible, specific rules for dogs are not detailed. Given the moderate difficulty and potential for technical sections, ensure your dog is fit for a long mountain bike ride. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or livestock, and to carry water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to access this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this trail. The Cañada Real Sancha Brava, which forms a significant part of the route, is a historic public pathway.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route is typically during spring or autumn. These seasons offer more moderate temperatures, which are ideal for a 43-kilometer ride. Summers in Extremadura can be very hot, and winters might bring temporary streams and potentially muddy conditions, especially in areas like the 'Cuesta Bermeja' with its clay-rich soil.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Natural Corredor Cáceres-Badajoz. Ruta Cáceres-Badajoz-Embalse de Piedra Aguda, the Camino Natural del Corredor Cáceres - Badajoz, and various segments of the Cañada Real Sancha Brava itself, among others like Camino del Cahoso and Camino de Serradilla a Cahoso.

faqs.question: Where can I find parking or trailhead access for this route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking locations are not detailed in the provided information. However, as a circular route, you would typically start and end at the same point. Look for public parking areas near the beginning of the Cañada Real Sancha Brava or close to the Mirador de Badajoz, which is a likely starting point for a route named after it.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, pubs, or accommodation options near the trail?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not specify cafes, pubs, or accommodation directly on the trail. As the route is near Badajoz, you would likely find amenities in the city itself or surrounding towns. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a mountain bike ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and 43-kilometer length, you should pack plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic bike repair kit (including a spare tube and pump), and appropriate sun protection. Wearing a helmet is essential. Depending on the season, consider layers of clothing and rain gear. A GPS device or the komoot app with the route downloaded is also highly recommended for navigation.
